question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getV1OpenApiFile()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated concurrent requests to any API endpoint before the module initializes, causing multiple simultaneous `fs.readFileSync` calls that block the Node.js event loop and prevent the mirror node from processing any new transaction confirmations?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getV1OpenApiFile()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a lock/mutex around the `if (isUndefined(v1OpenApiFile))` check in `getV1OpenApiFile()` to trigger a race condition where multiple concurrent requests each call `fs.readFileSync` synchronously, blocking the event loop long enough to cause a total service outage?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getOpenApiSpecObject()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial-of-service by sending requests that trigger `yaml.load()` on a large or deeply nested YAML spec file, exhausting CPU and memory and preventing the mirror node REST API from confirming any new transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getOpenApiMap()] Can an unprivileged external user crash the mirror node at startup by sending a request that triggers `getOpenApiMap()` initialization when the OpenAPI spec file is missing or malformed, causing an unhandled exception that terminates the process and shuts down transaction confirmation?
